rulings.Case Title: LONNETTE ROBINSON ET AL VS. LARUE PERKINS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 26, 2019 line 1. RESPONDENTS LARUE PERKINS, PATRICIA ANDERSON, BETTY BOWSER'S Petition to Vacate Arbitration Award GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to Part II (pages 8 to 10) of Arbitration award as it is applicable to utilizing partners occupying units in San Francisco. If an error appears on the face of an arbitration award that causes substantial injustice, the award may be vacated. The arbitrator correctly determined that utilizing partners' San Francisco tenancies are covered by the Rent Ordinance. Arbitrator's mistake of law/fact that "Rent Control Ordinance would not preclude imposition of a cost recovery provision" effectively resulting in 300%-600% rent increase (Rent Ordinance 37.2(a)(1), base rent; (p), rent; (q) rent increase) for long-term (1970s-2019) tenants causes substantial injustice. Utilizing Partners' occupancy predated 1994 Partnership Agreement. To the extent the agreement purports to increase the pre-existing rent, this portion of the agreement is void as against public policy (Rent Ordinance 37.9(e)). To the extent the agreement does not change the rent and reduces to writing the historical rent as being 30% of FMV, the rent is the legally allowable rent at the time of the execution of the Partnership agreement. (See Rent Ordinance 37.2(a), (p), q))The Court notes that the responded made a request to correct the award. If there is no objection from the petitioner, the Court would entertain this request. If there is an objection, the issue shall be addressed in any subsequently brought arbitration if necessary and proper. Motion is otherwise denied. There are no grounds to vacate any other parts of the award. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 26, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT STEPHEN HOM MOTION TO STRIKE 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to strike part of paragraph 37 is granted as to p.10 lines 12-13 with leave to amend for Plaintiff to file an amendment to Fifth Amended Complaint stating "Plaintiff is informed and believes that Stanley Hom, AS TRUSTEE, named as Doe 2 in Plaintiff's Amendment to Complaint Naming Does 1-4 is now deceased." Motion is otherwise denied. Judicial estoppel is not applicable under the circumstances of the dispute between the parties. The capacity and identity of persons liable for breaches of contracts and entitled to the benefits of the contracts are in the center of the dispute. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Objection And Objection To The Form, Substance, And Amount, And Sufficiency Thereof, Of The Judgment Debtor
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 26, 2019 line 8. PLAINTIFFS DALE DUNCAN, MARTA MUNOZ MENDOZA'S Motion In Support Of Objection And Objection To The Form, Substance, And Amount, And Sufficiency Thereof, Of The Judgment Debtor SUSTAINED. Procedural objections to the names, discrepancies in labelling of attachments etc. are well taken. Objections to the adequacy of the evaluations and sufficiency of value, ownership of property, clouds on title, and existence of the described property etc. are also well taken. The Court takes Judicial Notice of Plaintiff's Exhibits L, M, N, O, P, Q, R, S, T. Defendants have 5 days to give a bond with sufficient sureties and in a sufficient amount. (CCP §995.960(b)(1).) = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SUK CHAN LEE VS. JOI BRETHAUER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 26, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANTS JOI BRETHAUER, MICHAEL BRETHAUER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff should address the inconsistency raised by paragraph 8a and 7b of the First Amended Complaint, specifically that the 3 Day Notice to Pay Rent or Quit was served and expired on March 5, 2019. = (501/CFH)
